behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources (metrics, issue tracker, deployment log).
behaviors.justification: This tests data aggregation logic in a single function boundary. A mock-based Unit test mocks all three APIs to return fixed data, calls the collection function, and verifies the input passed to the summarizer includes results from all three sources. This catches cases where the code skips calling one source, calls it but ignores the result, or fails to aggregate correctly. No external I/O or services needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test mocking all three data sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker, deployment log) to return fixed payloads, calling the aggregation function, and asserting the summarizer input includes data from all three sources. Verifies all sources are called and their results are included.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This tests error-handling logic within the data-collection function. A Unit test mocks one source to raise an exception while the other two return normally, calls the function, and verifies: (1) the code does not abort, (2) a 'data unavailable' note is inserted for the failed source, (3) data from working sources is included. This catches missing or broken exception handlers. The core failure mode—incorrect error handling—is logic, not infrastructure. Small Integration hedge (0.2) accounts for possibility that real network failures (timeout, malformed response) might manifest differently than mocked exceptions.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that mocks one data source to raise an exception, mocks the other two to return valid data, calls the report-generation function, and asserts: report includes 'data unavailable' note, code completes without aborting, and other source data is present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on the real LLM's output quality, not just the code's logic. A Unit test with a mocked LLM can verify the code passes data correctly to the summarizer, but cannot verify the summary is actually accurate—that requires the real LLM. LLM output is non-deterministic and can vary in quality across runs (hallucinations, omissions, misrepresentations). The real failure mode is: the LLM generates a poor summary. To catch that, the test must call the real LLM with known inputs and verify the output is accurate. Small Unit hedge (0.2) for case where behavior is interpreted as 'code correctly passes data' rather than 'summary is accurate.'
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test that calls the real LLM with input data containing specific key values, verifies the generated narrative includes and accurately represents those values without hallucinations, and spot-checks for factual accuracy. Requires real LLM because summary quality varies with LLM non-determinism.

behaviors.description: A complete report—from data collection through email delivery—reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This end-to-end behavior requires real email delivery infrastructure. A Unit test with mocked SMTP can verify the code calls `send_email()`, but cannot verify the email actually reaches the inbox. Real SMTP failures (server unreachable, authentication failure, misconfiguration, network timeout) cannot be caught by in-process mocks. The behavior explicitly states the report 'reaches the inbox'—this requires real or test SMTP server infrastructure. System-level test uses either real email service (external) or local test SMTP (MailHog or similar), both of which involve real network I/O and infrastructure beyond the process boundary.
behaviors.test_description: System test (end-to-end) that exercises the full pipeline: calls real data sources (mocked or real), generates report with real or acceptable-mock LLM, sends email via real SMTP or test SMTP server (e.g., MailHog), and verifies the email appears in the configured recipient's inbox with correct content and structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This behavior involves three components that must work together: (1) SMTP failure detection and handling (logic), (2) report persistence to disk (file I/O), (3) scheduler retry on next run (time-based, external). A Unit test with mocked SMTP and mocked scheduler can verify error handling and save logic, but cannot verify the retry actually executes on schedule. The real failure modes include: SMTP times out in a way the code doesn't catch, the saved file is corrupted or unreadable on retry, the scheduler doesn't re-run the job. To catch these, you need real file I/O, real SMTP failure (network simulation or service down), and real or simulated scheduler behavior. System-level infrastructure is required.
behaviors.test_description: System test that triggers the report job, simulates SMTP unreachability (real network failure, service down, or mocked exception in an integration harness), verifies the report is saved to the configured file with correct content, waits for or manually triggers the next scheduled run, and asserts the retry succeeds and email is delivered once SMTP recovers.
